note: SessionPoolFailoverRunner boot-wiring (Multi-Machine Session Pool §Rollout, Track H) — the missing 3rd mesh piece. The merged pieces (SessionPoolFailoverRunner orchestrator + sessionPoolFailoverCheck subprocess check) existed but were constructed NOWHERE at server boot (grep SessionPoolFailoverRunner src/commands/server.ts returned nothing), so a deployed dev agent never produced its own honest failover green and its sessionPool sat at 'shadow' forever. This increment wires it, dark. New module src/core/sessionPoolFailoverRunnerConfig.ts adds the dev-gate config resolver (resolveSessionPoolFailoverRunnerConfig, mirroring resolveSingleMachineFailoverGapConfig), a stateful slow-cadence throttle (SessionPoolFailoverRunnerDriver) that owns the tickInterval gate + in-flight re-entrancy guard + the read-only status snapshot, and a construct-or-null factory (buildSessionPoolFailoverRunnerDriver). Boot-wired in server.ts INSIDE the existing rollout-gate try block next to SessionPoolE2EResultStore/StageAdvancer: constructs a REAL bounded runProcess (async cp.spawn of node_modules/.bin/vitest run <e2e> --reporter=dot; resolves the instar source root from INSTAR_SOURCE_ROOT|dist/../..|cwd; a deployed agent with no source/vitest yields ranToCompletion:false -> the check THROWS -> the runner records nothing = honest degrade), drives maybeTick() off the existing 60s stageReconcileTimer (self-throttled to its own 1h cadence, NOT a hot loop), holds a module-scope handle + a getSessionPoolFailoverRunner getter. dryRun-FIRST honesty: a recorded green PROMOTES the stage (real authority), so while dryRun holds the driver points the runner at a SIDE result store (session-pool-failover-runner-dryrun.json) the StageAdvancer never reads — nothing promotes until a deliberate dryRun:false; a THROW records nothing in either mode (never a fabricated verdict). New read route GET /session-pool/failover-runner (503 when dark, 200 status() when live). DARK-GATE TRAP HANDLED: the ConfigDefaults failoverRunner block OMITS enabled (rides resolveDevAgentGate, never a hardcoded false — the #1001 anti-pattern) so it adds NO attributed dark-gate row; it is a 15-line insert INSIDE the sessionPool block below holdForStability (1451), so it shifts the 4 golden-map rows below it (threadlinePairing 1697->1712, cartographer 1876->1891/1921->1936/1946->1961) uniformly by +15 — hand-bumped in tests/unit/lint-dev-agent-dark-gate.test.ts with a documenting comment (regeneration FORBIDDEN; RE-VERIFIED via attributeEnabledFalsePaths on the edited ConfigDefaults, still 25 entries, no new/removed). NOT guard-shaped (src/core/, file basename does not match the *Sentinel/*Detector suffix set, not in ADDITIONAL_CANDIDATES) — no guardManifest entry needed (lint-guard-manifest clean confirms). Migration parity: type + ConfigDefaults default + DEV_GATED_FEATURES entry (sessionPoolFailoverRunner) + CapabilityIndex session-pool prefix reason updated + strip-migration migrateConfigSessionPoolFailoverRunnerDevGate registered in migrateConfig (existence-checked, #1001 defense; the { dryRun, tickIntervalMs, checkTimeoutMs } block arrives via applyDefaults add-missing). All three test tiers: unit (resolver dev-gate/dryRun-first/cadence-floor + factory enabled→constructs/dark→null + honest recording to the right store dryRun→side/live→real + honest-degrade no-source→records-nothing + throttle skip), integration (route dark→503/live→200), e2e (real AgentServer feature-alive: enabled→200/dark→503/auth-required). Additive, dark-by-construction. JUDGMENT CALLS (flagged): (1) provenStage fixed at 0 (the shadow index StageAdvancer.advanceTo('live-transfer') reads) — v1 proves only the shadow→live-transfer failover, matching the merged runner's documented v1 scope; a later stage would parameterize this. (2) dryRun is honored by pointing the runner at a SIDE signed store rather than adding a dryRun branch to the merged runner (kept the merged core untouched); the promotion path reads only the real store, so a dry-run green is captured for the soak but can never promote. (3) The real runProcess spawns the actual two-server E2E as a subprocess — heavy, but exactly the merged design's intent (the injected runProcess in production), bounded by the 1h cadence + in-flight guard + 180s subprocess timeout + dark-by-default gate. tsc clean; 43/43 new-test green; full npm run lint clean (incl. lint-dev-agent-dark-gate + lint-guard-manifest); 359/359 green across the touched shared-file unit suites.
